verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is a single-pass type I transmembrane protein whose dominant localization is the ER membrane, where it functions as a luminal Ca2+ sensor gating ORAI channels. However, a well-documented, constitutive minority pool (~25% in myeloid cells) resides at the plasma membrane with its N-terminal EF-SAM domain facing the extracellular space, confirmed by flow cytometry, surface biotinylation, and intact-cell antibody staining. This STIM1PM pool has its own TM anchor at the PM and plays roles in store-independent Ca2+ entry and tumor suppression. Because the ER is the dominant compartment but a genuine PM pool with extracellular accessibility is constitutively present across multiple cell types, this is a dual_localization contextual call.
